entry.title: Creating side-by-side process extensions from SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
The release of SAP Build's new extensibility wizard forSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release marks a significant step in streamlining the creation of on-stack extensions (within SAP S/4HANA Cloud) and side-by-side extensions (in SAP BTP) to help keep the core clean. The wizard, which can be accessed directly from any business application that is enabled for key user adaptation, allows key users and even developers to easily create custom process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) that can seamlessly integrate with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. In this blog, we will explore the use cases for process extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and how the new wizard is a game-changer for customers looking to enhance their business processes

Side-by-side extensions

Side-by-side extensions run outside of SAP S/4HANA Cloud (or other business application) but interact with the business applications using standard extension points like APIs, events, etc. Side-by-side extensions can technically run on any application platform, nevertheless SAP provides and recommends its dedicated platform for extension: SAP BTP.

What are process extensions

Many applications in SAP S/4HANA use SAP Business Workflow enabling preconfigured workflow scenarios to be reused in various situations. The scenarios can either be implemented without any changes or configured for your business processes by making minor adjustments. On top of that, Flexible workflows allow you to define condition-based workflows for the pre-configured approval processes. For example, the flexible workflow for purchase requisitions allows you to define one-step or multi-step approval processes for purchase requisitions according to your requirements. These are pre-delivered as part of the SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. However, your use case may go beyond a typical pre-delivered approval workflow which needs a side-by-side process extension. Here are some use case patterns which warrants a process extension in SAP BTP:

  • Processes that span across systems.
  • Processes that reach the users who are outside SAP S/4HANA.
  • Use certain capabilities from SAP BTP, like SAP AI Services.
  • Any other requirements that are not covered in the standard processes and workflows.

Example use case

Consider a specific use case where you want to make sure that every invoice created in the system has a reference to a purchase order without user intervention. There are multiple ways this can be achieved including a process extension. A side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (in SAP BTP) can check if a purchase order is assigned for every new supplier invoice created in the system. If not, then the process shall identify the right purchase order using SAP AI Services in SAP BTP like Document Information Extraction, Data Attribute Recommendation, Invoice Object Recommendation, SAP AI Core, etc. When the machine learning model could not find the right match, then it could inform the procurement department member for further action. This process extension would run alongside the standard procurement process, adding the necessary functionality without altering the original process.

Why SAP Build Process Automation?

SAP Build Process Automation in SAP BTP with its rich development tools, integration capability and pre-built content is the recommended process extension platform for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. SAP Build Process Automation has 500+ pre-built content packages for extending SAP and non-SAP applications. Most of those content is free-to-use and can be accessed from SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition 2408 release, the extensibility wizard streamlines the creation of process extensions in SAP Build Process Automation. The process created in SAP Build Process Automation is deployed & executable in SAP BTP and can be triggered automatically when a business event is observed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

Creating a process extension

Let us see how easy it is to create a process extension with the new wizard.

Pre-requisite:

  • Setup SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh

A business event-based triggering of process is a capability of SAP Build Process Automation. It requires SAP Event Mesh or SAP Advanced Event Mesh to observe the events in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to initiate a process in SAP Build Process Automation. Refer to this blog for the detailed setup instruction.

  • You have the right business role and can access the extensibility wizard as described in previous blog of this series.

Procedure

  • From any application which is enabled for key user adaptation, open the 'Me Area' to choose the new 'Create Extension' option to launch the extensibility wizard.

Choose Process Extension. (If you do not have entitlement to SAP Build Process Automation in the connected SAP BTP tenant, then this option will be disabled with a 'Not subscribed' ribbon in the tile).

Based on the application from where the extension was triggered, the wizard automatically identifies the available extension points (in 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition, only business events are supported. More extension points is planned as future increments).

Choose Business Events, and proceed to next step.

The wizard also recognises the event objects that are relevant for the application and automatically applies a filter on the business object. Of course, you can override these filters and search any event that is needed for your use case. Select the appropriate event object and proceed to the next step.

All events of the selected event object are shown. Select the appropriate event and proceed to the next step.

Give a name and description for your extension project that will be created in SAP Build (SAP BTP). 

Confirm the changes in the Summary step or go back to edit any steps. This creates an extension project for based in the inputs given in the previous steps.

Once the project is created successfully, you can directly navigate to the project in SAP Build. 

When navigated into SAP Build (in SAP BTP), you can see a process automation project with a process created for you. 

When you open the process, you can see that the event selected in the wizard is automatically added. All the context from the selected business event is now available at your disposal. 

You can proceed to use the low-code process builder to create an extension process as needed. You can call any APIs like the SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP Cloud Integration models or any SAP BTP services, you can add a user task that creates a task in My Inbox or SAP Task Center, you can call an RPA bot automation, you can send notification emails, etc. Once the process is created, release a version and deploy the content into SAP BTP. 

When this process automation project is deployed in SAP BTP, it listens to the specific event in the connected SAP S/4HANA Cloud. When such an event is observed, it starts an instance of the process with the context that is available in the business event.

For more information on the lifecycle, refer to the help documentation of SAP Build Process Automation.

Key take aways

  • A brand-new wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is available with 2408 release to create on-stack and side-by-side extensions.
  • The wizard can be called from any application in SAP S/4HANA Cloud public edition that is enabled for key user adaptation.
  • Discover the available process extension points based on the application that was used to start the wizard, through the concept of Business Context.
  • Create a side-by-side process extension in SAP Build Process Automation (SAP BTP) directly from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Look-ahead plans

  • Management of side-by-side extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  • More extension points for process extensions apart from the business event.
  • Enable the wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition.

entry.title: Re: Material Analysis Report
entry.content:
I agree with DominikTylczyn : Material Analysis and other types of Inventory Controlling reports have evolved with S/4HANA. It would be better to focus on the new possibilities / limitations ...

Link : 3126267 - What are successor Fiori apps for Inventory Controlling (Application 03) report like MC.9 in S/4HANA system? - SAP for Me

Kind regards

Nic T.

entry.title: Re: Outbound SMS / WhatsApp Message from SAP Service Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Kapil,

In our company we are using C4C and also have some experience with Twilio and also with Sinch.

I guess C4C's OData API would do just fine in this case + some simple middleware on Linux would be sufficient.

Roman

BR Daniel

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content: Hi my name is Katlego and I'm a 38 year old job seeker with Bi-polar who has 3 years programming experience in Delphi. Now I have my interests is peaked on end-user programming which is why I'm keen on embarking on the digital skills program and hopefully becoming an SAP consultant.

entry.title: i am using custom v2 service mdk after that i am getting HTTP status from the back end is 508
entry.content: am using custom v2 service for btp mdk after that i am getting HTTP status from the back end is 508 error. checked the logs in that .error message is Failed to access backend with error Request failed with status code 508, please check your destination in SAP Mobile Services cockpit..

entry.title: Re: call classe methods below in another class code
entry.content: Use a CLASS cl_control DEFINITION DEFERRED before using it in the first class.

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Datasphere with SAP Integrated Business Planning, using SAP CI-DS - Part 3
entry.content:
Hi Domnic,

very good blog series, thanks a lot for your efforts. I have two questions about this option (Integrating via CI-DS using ODBC)

  1. Am I right in the assumption that by using CI-DS technically the connection from CI-DS to Datasphere is made by the SAP Data Services Agent acting as an SAp HANA client?
  2. Hence all traffic will effectively routed like this?
    • (IBP triggers CI-DS e,g. per Job) --> CI-DS --> Agent --> Datasphere --> Agent --> CI-DS --> IBP
  3. Regarding licenses: Does Datasphere license allow a HANA Client to connect to Datasphere? I know that SAP made quite a fuss for the on-premise HANA DB by separating full use license and application license, e.g. when having a BW/4HANA deployment. In my book for an on-premise BW/4HANA deployment one would need a HANA full use license to connecto to the HDB with a HANA client. I would be happy if SAP would allow this on Datasphere, though 🙂

Many thanks and kind regards

Jens

entry.title: ArchiveLink Attachments with Harmonized Document Management for Request for Quotations
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
im trying to use ArchiveLink attachments (via HDM) within the Fiori app F2049.
Currently i'm only able to use DMS attachments. I hope someone can help me with this.

The configuration table /view (V_AO_TYPE_DEF) includes the Objecttype 'RequestForQuotation', the object type 'BUS2010' and the TCLO-object 'EKKO_RFQ'.

Within the HDM_CUST i cannot activate ArchiveLink attachments as i get an warning that ArchiveLink is not valid.

Best Regards,
Julian

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM, tenant extension now available on the SAP Price List
entry.content:

BR
Nicolas

entry.title: Re: REV.EXE file usage in SAP work directory
entry.content:
Hi Selle,

I checked in my SAP system which is running Windows 2016 OS with MS Sql 2016, I can't find the file name rev.exe, so you can simply move the file from SAP folder to some other temp folder, and then if possible, do the full system restart so that it will cleared your query.

Regards

SS

entry.title: File download no longer working in CAP 8
entry.content:
Hi community,

we are in the progress of migrating our CAP7 application to CAP8. In our app we have a function to download an asset from the BTP object store defined like this:

    this.on(download, async (req: Request) => {
      const fileBuffer = await this.objectStoreService.read(
        `assets/${req.data.assetId}`,
      );

      req.http?.res.setHeader('Content-Type', asset.mediaType!);
      req.http?.res.setHeader(
        'Content-Disposition',
        `inline;filename=${asset.fileName}`,
      );
      req.http?.res.send(fileBuffer);
    });

In CAP7 this code worked fine, but now it's giving the following error:

[cds] - Error: Cannot set headers after they are sent to the client

Actually, even the following simple code will now give the error above.

this.on(download, async (req: Request) => {
  req.http?.res.send('OK');
}

Any recommendations on how to implement a file download with CAP8 now?

Thanks

Pedro

entry.title: SAP Integrated Business Planning for Inventory Fundamentals
entry.content:
Are you looking to optimize your inventory management and improve your supply chain performance? If so, we have just the e-learning course for you. Introducing SAP Integrated Business Planning for Inventory Fundamentals offered by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy.

This comprehensive e-learning course will provide you with the knowledge and skills you need to effectively manage your inventory targets, balance risk, and maximize customer service levels. Whether you are new to SAP IBP for inventory as part of SAP Digital Supply Chain or looking to expand your knowledge, this course is designed to meet your needs.

Throughout this course, you will learn about inventory target calculation options and factors, basic data requirements, the impact of forecast error on safety stock, and much more. You will also gain valuable insights into inventory optimization key fundamentals and learn how to set up workflows to execute inventory optimization in your system.

By enrolling in this course, you will gain an overview of right-sizing inventory, understand how to calculate safety stocks, and learn how to effectively utilize IBP-Inventory to improve your inventory management processes.

The course covers the following topics:

  • Inventory target calculations and factors
  • Basic data requirements
  • Impact of demand variability on inventory target
  • Multi-stage concept
  • Impact of supply variability on inventory target

With our easy-to-follow, self-paced e-learning format, you can access the course material at your convenience and learn at your own pace. Our experienced instructors will guide you through the content, providing you with real-world examples and practical insights that you can apply to your own business.

So, if you are ready to take your inventory management skills to the next level, enroll in our SAP Integrated Business Planning for Inventory Fundamentals e-learning course today. Join us and gain the knowledge and expertise you need to optimize your inventory management and drive success in your supply chain.

Please note:
Before you can access the link to the e-learning course, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

 Click here to access the e-learning:

SAP Integrated Business Planning for Inventory Fundamentals

entry.title: Re: Asset Write-up (ABZU) for Prior Fiscal year in Prior...
entry.content:
If you can't find a suitable transaction code

  • (which ones did you try and what error(s) did you get?)

You could allow negative depreciation (select all allowed values) in OABN and create a transaction code by copying unplanned depreciation (640 of the asset is acquired in previous years) and use it?

entry.title: Looking for app developers who monitor apps they deployed
entry.content:
Hi,

We're working on a new dashboard for application monitoring for frontend, backend, and fullstack developers. 

I'm looking for developers who monitor the application they deployed for short interview to better understand what info you would expect to see and what actions you would expect to be available for you.

No need to prepare anything. Only meet for 30 min interview and impact on dashboard design.

Your feedback is highly appreciated.

Ravid (ravid.ellenbogen@sap.com)

entry.title: Re: Find API based interfaces in a SAP system in question
entry.content: This is not an answer to your question, right? In order to clarify your question, you should use a "Comment", not an "Answer"...

entry.title: Looking for app developers who monitor apps they deployed
entry.content:
Hi,

We're working on a new dashboard for application monitoring for frontend, backend, and fullstack developers. 

I'm looking for developers who monitor the application they deployed for short interview to better understand what info you would expect to see and what actions you would expect to be available for you.

No need to prepare anything. Only meet for 30 min interview and impact on dashboard design.

Your feedback is highly appreciated.

Ravid (ravid.ellenbogen@sap.com)

entry.title: Re: Story filter becomes open for user modification after...
entry.content:
@ivan_camac 

Hi Ivan,

Thanks for the comment. I understand your point of view, but then I would argue that if we count interactions through the API as user-edits, then it shouldn't be possible at all, as the filter is set to be non-editable. 

In our organisation, story users and story designers are two separate groups. We use the API if we want to perform some actions "behind the scenes" to ensure that the less proficient users have easy access to the data they need.

In other stories we do similar setup to what you mentioned - we block the access to the filters completely, and manage everything through the API. But in this case, the mixed access seemed to make more sense. Unfortunately it is not possible due to the unexpected API behaviour, so for now we will hide all the filters completely. 

Cheers, Łukasz

Hari

entry.title: Re: SOAP using communication arrangement
entry.content: Thanks for your help! So, soap actions are not available with communication arrangement. For me, it's at least a problem and maybe an error. This issue was already solved 20 years ago using soamanager and some extra configuration 😞 Regarding the http protocol, I know it's not commonly used in real services, but anyway, I guess it should be possible to use or explicitly forbidden.

entry.title: Set Transport time using ChaRM in Solman
entry.content:
Is it possible to set the transport time using ChaRM in SAP Solution Manager?
If possible, could you provide step-by-step procedure?

entry.title: Re: Asset Viewer Object Navigator Config
entry.content: Hi Tim, Thank you for your reply. I have seen these BF's are currently deactivated so i will activate and look again. Much appreciated with the help, Regards Tyrone

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP: How to add query parameters to S/4 backend call
entry.content:
I suppose these parameters are fixed for a connection. If so, you can add the following additional properties to the destination.

URL.queries.BackendUser=00010000000003
URL.queries.Scenario=D

The parameters  will get added to the URL as query parameters.

entry.title: Re: Due date
entry.content:
Could you checl or paste the Terms of Payment definitions (V_T052, Tr. OBB8 or SPRO)

  • Also use search tool, there are already some blogs on this subject

entry.title: Query : How to Deny : SAML2=Disabled
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We have a URL host publicly accessible over the internet after SAML SSO authentication.

Client has raised a concern after knowing that SAML SSO can be bypassed by adding the parameter "saml2=disabled".

Is there any way we can enforce SAML authentication even if someone uses "saml2=disabled" option.

 

thanks,
Rajat

entry.title: Re: SOAP using communication arrangement
entry.content: No more help related this issue?

entry.title: Is it possible to set-up promotion to give only discounted prices for 3 products per customer acc?
entry.content:
We have a business requirement to set-up discounted prices per customer account but only limit to 3 products per account on the promo period.

So for the 4th product order and succeeding orders, the price will revert to original.

Is this possible for OOTB promotion templates? How to set it up? 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: ATP check for sto purchase orders
entry.content:
Hello Andreas, Thank you for prompt reply my question is that is there any chance to check for purchase orders something like this where we can see elaborated details for purchase orders 

eg :below same for purchase order as for sscui which is mentioned for you can only see details as display cannot eloborate further .

entry.title: Re: How to obtain Valuation Type from Stock in Transit?
entry.content:
Hello @piniguez 

Stock in transit is managed in MSTB table. Valuation type is in the BWTAR field.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Multi-User Mode in mdk
entry.content:
Hello experts,

i've recently read Enable Multi-User Mode for MDK Application | SAP Tutorials tutorial and implemented it in my app.

And it seems to fairly work while i am using the Mobileservices client, but seem to be having problems in my .apk. 

I've alerted the clientAPI.isAppInMultiUserMode() result and i get different results. For mobileservices client i get true, but in my apk i get false. Do i have to do something different when implementing this for an android package. Is this functionality supported outside of Mobile services Client?

 

Thank you for your support, Raul

entry.title: Responsibility Management: Creating custom teams & responsibility definitions in S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
Hi All,

As we move towards a clean core and an intelligent enterprise, we need a standard framework which can alert the user about the tasks which need their attention. With Responsibility management in S/4 HANA, SAP provides a standard set of apps which will be used to configure teams, assign members and create agent determination rules for standard as well as custom workflows.

OVERVIEW

Common Approaches:

In any S/4 HANA project, there are multiple scenarios where customers need to determine agents for approval of purchase order, invoices, sales order, billing document, Master data creation/change etc. For each of these processes we use different ways to determine agents, like BADI, BRF+ and custom tables.

Challenges:

All of these methods of determining agents depend on IT teams for configuration and coding, as well as not very user friendly for the end user to setup agent determination rules. The current approaches are not very scalable, especially with side-by-side scenarios running SAP BTP, ABAP environment 

In comes Responsibility management.... With this SAP provides a standard framework to create teams and assign members for various scenarios running either on stack or side by side on SAP BTP.

For more details, please refer to the standard documentation here

 

MAIN CONTENT

In the scenario which we will cover today, the workflow runs in SAP Build Process Automation and the teams are created in S/4 HANA and exposed via ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model API.

But before that a quick overview of the terms to be used in the blog

Team category: It is the classification of team by business processes like Finance, supply chain etc.

Team Type: Each team category can have multiple team types, it's breakdown of team by business process like, Finance team for USA and Finance team for UK.

Responsibility Attributes: The attributes based on which teams will be created and members will be assigned to the team.

There are standard team types, team category & responsibility definitions which SAP provides to cater to different scenarios, however in my case I have created custom responsibility definitions, team category and team types.

1. Create a custom team category using Manage team categories app. The team category name will be prefixed by default by identifier which is configured in ABAP transport organizer (ATO). For my case it was ZZ1.

2. Create a team type and assign it to the custom team category.

3. Create custom responsibility definition. To create custom responsibility definition, you have to create CDS views and assign them to the team category.

2. This CDS view has 2 backend tables, one for code and other for text. On top of these 2 tables, 2 CDS views are created.

4.  Function and Function profile needs to be created, this is required to assign the team members to a particular team. The functions can also be used for controlling authorizations, however in this case we are only using functions as a pre-requisite step to create members of a team.

5. Create the team using Manage team and responsibilities app. Once you select a particular team type automatically all the responsibilities assigned to the team category will automatically show up and you can provide the necessary values.

Each of the team member will be business partner. For each SU01 user a corresponding BP needs to be created, then only it will show up in this app.

6. These teams are stored in backend tables but for easy access there are standard data definitions. These can be used in your ABAP code.

7. To expose these teams to external applications which are running side-by-side your S/4 HANA core, standard API's are available. Detailed documentation can be found here.

8. In our case we have created a RAP API using custom entity, to extract the team's data along with some additional parameters and send it to SBPA via a pull mechanism.

PS: The details of RAP API are not part of this blog.

CONCLUSION:

Responsibility management provides a one stop solution for all your agent determination needs. It can help consolidate process and application specific agent determination requirements into a single application. It would help in removing data silos for agent determination rules, provides a scalable framework which can be used across SAP applications with proper authorizations.

Please free to share your thoughts and reach out to me if you have any questions.

SAP S/4HANA SAP BTP, ABAP environment SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition  ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: Re: Need to know the default host creation.
entry.content: @StephiyaLobo if this answer served you well, please mark this answer as the solution. This will close this question.

entry.title: Job History view for previously in target Employees
entry.content:
Hi,

We are looking for an approach to cover the following scenario:

  • Employee A has been hired from 2020 to 2023 in a US legal entity
  • from 1.1.2023 the employee has been moved to Italy (terminated in US and re-hired in Italy)

HRs have a RBP with a target on specific legal entity and the HR in US, from 1.1.2023 cannot view anymore the information related to the employee who has been moved.

When navigating to the profile, he has not an HR view on his data, neither changing the effective as of date of visualization and, moreover, when HR makes reports with effective date e.g. in 2021 the employee does not appear for US HR because he is not in the target anymore.

Any suggestion on how to fix the situation?

entry.title: Re: How to obtain Valuation Type from Stock in Transit?
entry.content: Hello @DominikTylczyn. Unfortunately, that table is empty in our system. Do you know of any alternative? Thanks again

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS Releases CDS-Native Data Types: CDS Simple Types
entry.content:
@somnath 

I could find below additional advantages/ differences. 

  1. Data elements cannot be nested but only copied for the reusability purpose where as CDS simple type.
  2. CDS Simple type is a basic version of ABAP CDS - Enumerated Types

Best wishes,

Ramjee Korada

entry.title: Generate video with sound
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm generating a video out of a simulation but the video is missing the audio output, i.e. there is no sound, although the demo has audio and the text-to-speech is generated.

We have installed the latest version of Producer - 10.8.4 and I tried again. I managed to produce a video with sound, but only for a screencast video. And in order to have that option, the recording should be done with Profile 'Quick recording' - the only one allowing to Record Screencast Video.

I have to insert some book pages, but when I do that and try to generate a video I get the following options:

The problem is that if I choose Simulation Video, I get a video with the book pages plus the steps I recorded, but no sound.

If  I choose Screencast Video, I get sound but the book pages are not shown - only the recorded steps are included.

My questions are:

1. Is a video with audio output foreseen to work only for Screencast video (i.e. Quick Recording profile)? Can I have the audio output in a Simulation video as well?

2. Can I insert book pages in a screencast video?

Many thanks!

Roumiana

entry.title: Re: Any Note so that the External Equipment Type accepts...
entry.content:
SAP systems can work with many languages and character sets, but there may be restrictions. The "Ñ" character, common in Western European languages like Spanish, may not be supported in your SAP system's configuration or there could be a system bug. I did not found any specific SAP note for this issue in Mexico.
The best will be to open a support incident for help.

entry.title: Re: How to create custom button in TMG
entry.content:
Thank you a lot for sharing this. This is exactly the guide I need for the latest requirement I've got. 

But there's still a concern that it will be deleted once someone re-generate the maint view is still quite uneasy to prevent. 

 

If anyone read past my message and have any suggestion, could you please leave your message. Thank you in advance! 🙏🙏🙏

entry.title: SAPB1 HANA - steps to import SAP Business One mobile cert for IOS 17
entry.content: Hi, is there any difference in the steps to import SAP Business One mobile cert for IOS 17 compared to the IOS older version?

entry.title: Re: Multi-User Mode in mdk
entry.content:
Hi @RaulM 

Do you have the MultiUserSettings enabled in your local BrandedSettings.json file?

"MultiUserSettings": {
        "MultiUserEnabled": true
      }

Make sure that you have it in your local project before you zip the .mdkproject directory to generate the branded client. I am assuming that you're using Mobile Cloud Build for generating the branded client (.apk).

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to activate background RFC for AIF Processing in your S/4HANA Central Finance?
entry.content:
Thank you for the helpful post. I have a question regarding rfc-destination. Which "Logon & Security" information should the destination have? Should the slt user be stored or set the "current user"-checkbox?

best regards

Soufian

entry.title: Re: REV.EXE file usage in SAP work directory
entry.content: Hi SS, thank you for providing input regarding this matter. As a matter of fact, this SAP system is also running Windows 2016 OS with MS Sql 2016. However, I need further details as to why this file occurred as requested by IT head.

entry.title: Re: Find API based interfaces in a SAP system in question
entry.content:
RFC-enabled function modules (RFMs) can be listed either using the F4-button in SE37, or by executing function module RFC_FUNCTION_SEARCH.

If your system is using UCON, however, then not all these FMs are callable from the outside. A standard ERP system contains about 38000 RFMs, but depending on which applications are actually used by your company, only a few hundred of them are needed. In order to reduce the security risk of remotely callable APIs that are not needed for production, SAP provides the UCON framework. It allows remote execution only of those few hundred FMs that are needed, and blocks incoming calls of the rest.

If UCON has been activated in your system, you can see the list of all allowed FMs as follows:

  • Go to transaction UCONCOCKPIT --> Navigate to Extended Selection
  • Mark the three radio buttons "All Function Modules", "RFMs Assigned to Default CA" and "All Phases"
  • Click the "Execute Selection" button

entry.title: Re: "Column store error" in database frequently
entry.content:
The 'GetRecords : column store error: search table error: [1000002] exception 1000002:' error message usually means there's a memory problem. This could be due to not enough memory or a memory leak.

What you can try:
- Make sure your system has enough memory for the database size. If not, increase the memory or upgrade your hardware.
- Use SAP HANA Studio or Cockpit to monitor memory usage and find any issues.
- Memory leaks can make your system run out of memory. Use SAP HANA tools to check for leaks.
- Reduce your database size by archiving or deleting old data, compressing data, or optimizing your database schema.
- If you're always running out of memory, you might need to upgrade your hardware. This could mean adding more RAM, upgrading your CPU, or increasing storage.

You can shrink the database size, but it depends on your data and its structure. You can archive, compress, or partition data to reduce the database size. But be careful to avoid data loss or performance problems.

For hardware specs, check the SAP HANA Hardware Directory and SAP Notes for the latest information. The requirements can change based on your specific needs and workload.

entry.title: Re: Add custom GUI Status AT Maintaining ztable (SM30)
entry.content:
SOLVED!

If you have a similar requirements. you can visit this link 

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/how-to-create-custom-button-in-tmg/ba-p/13281007

entry.title: Re: Using GNU tools to quickly check your input files - duplicates lines
entry.content:
Finding duplicates, very relevant!

sort [filename] | uniq -dc > duplicate_lines_please_check.txt

entry.title: Re: Is there way enable and disable tile and catalog thro...
entry.content: I understand that roles allow me to enable or disable specific tiles. However, I want this to occur based on certain events. For example, when a user selects a checkbox in the application, I want a specific tile in the work zone to be enabled. While I can assign an XSUAA role to the user, I'm unable to assign a work zone role directly.

entry.title: Re: Replicate transactions from one schema to another
entry.content: SAP HANA offers data replication options like System Replication for high-availability, Smart Data Integration (SDI) for data integration from various sources, and Landscape Transformation Replication Server (SLT) for real-time data replication from SAP and non-SAP sources. For transaction-level replication, consider SLT or SDI. As I know, you can filter and schedule replications, but careful planning is needed to avoid impacting system performance.

entry.title: Re: Issue displaying data in item list in Build Apps
entry.content:
Dear LM,

Beacause you are passing ID parameter for calling the Balance page that is why its showing only a single record. Do change the parameter and use only approved parameter. Also make the addition of "approved" field in the data set entitty which update when approving the Invoice.

Hope this will work.

regards,

M.Shakir

entry.title: Re: REV.EXE file usage in SAP work directory
entry.content:
Hi Selle1,

The REV.EXE file is used to reverse documents in SAP, such as invoices, credit memos, purchase orders, and more. And if it is in SAP standard directory then it should be a legitimate file.

Regards,

Prithviraj

entry.title: Re: Introduction to SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery service for SAP CPI Cloud Foundry enviro

Hi Shiveta,

Are the "META-INF", "src" directories, as well as the "metainfo.prop" file on root level of the repository? From the screenshot you gave it looks to me as if they are in a directory called "New_Test1". If you could move everything to the root level of the repository and try again.

Thanks and kind regards,
Oliver

entry.title: Re: Fiori launchpad integrated GPT assistant.
entry.content: Seems like a fun project. Congrats.

entry.title: Re: Reduce the number fo messages for BP-Replication
entry.content:
In DRFF you have two types of filters:
"Filter Criteria to Include/Exclude Business Objects"
and
"Segment Filters"

in your case use the first one (defines which objects to send),
the second type "reduces the data sent"

entry.title: Re: How to settle AuC to statistical internal order
entry.content:
As I know, settling an Asset Under Construction (AUC) to a statistical internal order isn't standard. It's typically settled to a real internal order or cost center.

 

I guess the best will be to ask SAP consulting team for clear answer or a workaround.

entry.title: Re: Can we overwrite the changes in existing FSV?
entry.content:
It seems you can't directly overwrite an existing Financial Statement Version (FSV) to prevent possible system errors.
To update an FSV, it appears you need to manually edit the original or delete and rename a new one, but it's advisable to check dependencies first.
It's recommended to test changes in a non-production environment to avoid potential unexpected issues.

entry.title: Re: REV.EXE file usage in SAP work directory
entry.content: Hi Prithviraj, thank you for your inputs regarding this matter. Would you know how this file is generated? This file only exists in this SAP system in comparison to others.

entry.title: Change-state in goal plan template and how to use this in the performance form at route map level
entry.content: How to work with change-state in goal plan template? Suppose, we have integrated goals in a performance form template, and in one step the goal is locked. Now, in the next step, we want the goal to be unlocked and the employee or manager should be able to enter goals in the form. Simultaneously, the goal is visible in the goal plan template as well? Kindly suggest how to do it.

entry.title: Re: [SAP B1] Double-click doesn't work for cell selection from displayed tables(list )
entry.content: did you get a solution?

entry.title: Re: How to obtain Valuation Type from Stock in Transit?
entry.content:
Hello @piniguez 

Check if you can see your stock in transit in MARC-UMLMC, where MARC-WERKS is the receiving plant. If so, then the valuation type can be read from MBEW.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: When I run MD04, I'm getting a lot of exception messa...
entry.content:
The MD04 exception message 07, "finish date in the past", typically means the system has identified an operation or order's finish date as past due. This could be due to incorrect scheduling parameters, production delays, or wrong master data.

Check below:
- Verify the scheduling parameters in transaction OPJH for the order type. Ensure the scheduling type is "Backward" for scheduling from the requirement date backwards.
- If production is delayed, reschedule the order to update production dates.
- Confirm the accuracy of the materials and work centers' master data, including routing, bill of materials, and work center capacities.
- Manually reschedule the order in transaction CO02 to recalculate dates based on current date and scheduling parameters.
- After corrections, rerun MRP to see if the issue is resolved.

entry.title: Re: Multi-User Mode in mdk
entry.content: Hello, yes i am using cloud build. But i dont have a BrandedSettings.json. Where can i edit this? Do i add it in the project manualy? If so, at application.app level?

Thanks,

Ahmed

entry.title: SAP Build Work Zone (SAP BTP, SAP Build Work Zone standard edition, free plan)
entry.content:
History of the problem:

  • An app was developed in SAP BAS and deployed to Cloud foundry
  • The App was included in SAP Build Work Zone content, but it turned out that intent navigation was incorrectly configured (As per note 2965719)
  • The app was undeployed corrected in SAP BAS, then deployed again. This was done several times because semanticObject and action settings did not seem to take effect
  • At some point the content channel could not be updated any more due to error described below
  • At the moment the app is undeployed from Cloud Foundry but still shows up in content channel, without the semanticObject and action as in the first incorrect version

 

The app is currently undeployed:

 

 

 

  1. Error when updating HTML5 Apps content channel:

 

Despite the error, status is set as Updated:

 

There is nothing in the report:

 

Downloaded JSON:

{

  "status": "SUCCEEDED",

  "contents": {

    "total": 0,

    "failed": 0,

    "status": "SUCCEEDED",

    "messages": {},

    "correlationId": "844ad7fa-30c6-4fd6-58c4-6da0e3292ad7"

  },

  "reportId": {

    "contextId": "SUB_ACCOUNT",

    "subdomain": "sb9br8n12566y8fr",

    "instanceId": "SAAS_APPROUTER_HTML5_APP_REPO",

    "providerId": "saas_approuter",

    "contextType": "sub_account",

    "identityZoneId": "66aa3fb8-d4b8-4433-992e-0aa841ced882"

  },

  "correlationId": "844ad7fa-30c6-4fd6-58c4-6da0e3292ad7"

}

The undeployed app is still in visible in content explorer:

 

 

  1. Error when saving local copy of the (non-existing) original app:

entry.title: Re: Where is data stored maintained in SAP Build Apps (from Visual Cloud Functions or from the App)
entry.content: Hi, you should be able use c4c apis directly by configuring them as btp destinations: see https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/btp-destinations-and-sap-build-apps-to-integrate-sap-c4c-s-4hana/ba-p/13551925

entry.title: Re: Where is data stored maintained in SAP Build Apps (from Visual Cloud Functions or from the App)
entry.content: So basically you can deside on what data source to use as long as it provides an api that can be accessed with BTP Destinations. Visual Cloud Functions is one option too, or your cap/rap apis.

entry.title: Re: Unit of Measure 'PCE' Is Not ISO Code - Where Has It Come From?
entry.content: @Donatien: Thanks for your replay. How can it be solved? Is there a recommendataion or update available? We need H87 as iso code for an integration scenario. But we just have PC or PCE als IDs available in SAP ERP ECC EHP8.

entry.title: Re: Introducing Remote Authorizations from SAP BW/4HANA for SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello Heiko,

THANK YOU for this very good description of the transfer of BW analysis authorizations to the DWC or DSP.

You write that hierarchy nodes are flattened and transferred as a single list of characteristic values.
Hierarchy node analysis authorizations are essential for us, as our two most important authorization-relevant characteristics 0ORGUNIT and 0PROFIT_CTR are used for this in BW4 reporting.
As you probably know, 0ORGUNIT is version-dependent, time-dependent and structurally time-dependent (Keyword: temporal hierarchy join at runtime of the report!) with regard to hierarchy definition in the business content standard delivery.
Hence the first question: Is the parameter regarding temp. hierarchy join fully supported for structurally time-dependent hierarchies (when transferring analysis authorizations from BW4 to DSP)?

On the other hand, we have intervals for the hierarchy nodes at 0PROFIT_CTR (parameter: Intervals permitted in hierarchy), i.e. a hierarchy node can consist of a PRCTR interval.
2nd question: Is this parameter also fully supported when flattening the hierarchy node?

What is the situation with compounded characteristics for authorization-relevant characteristics?
3rd question: Is this taken into account? 0PROFIT_CTR has 0CO_AREA and 0SEM_CGPRCT as compounded characteristics (in the business content of this InfoObject in the standard).

Finally, a special feature. Our users are not authorized to access all underlying characteristics (0PROFIT_CTR) of a hierarchy node. This means that we additionally use a Z-characteristic ZPRFT_CTR (with reference to 0PROFIT_CTR) in the analysis authorizations and a customer exit variable for a Z-ADSO with the individual PRCTR authorizations to additionally check this at runtime. This means that the hierarchy nodes in the analysis authorizations for 0PROFIT_CTR and the individual values of a hierarchy node are checked “simultaneously” using ZPRFT_CTR. When flattening the hierarchy nodes as you mentioned, this would mean that we would have to clean up the resolved (flattened) single values again for those that are not contained in the single authorizations of ZPRFT_CTR. Is this correct?

I would be very pleased to receive an answer.
Many THANKS in advance!

entry.title: SQL: retrieve the most recent entry
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to retrieve the most recent entry of my data table at once.

Let's imagine I have the following table (a piece of the MSEG table):

 

ROWSMATNRCHARGCPU_DATECPU_TIME
1000012345CA00109.16.202414:00:00
2000012345CA00209.16.202415:12:12
3000012345BO25603.03.202423:12:00
4000012345CU12309.17.202400:34:37
5000012345RA69827.06.202408:00:00
6000065887TT000123.03.202305:32:00
7000065887YU365401.01.202407:12:00
8000065887PP554627.12.202315:23:00

For article 000012345 I wanted to retrieve line 4 from an internal table and for article 000065887 line 7, which are the most recent records by comparison of the CPU_DATE/CPU_TIME fields.

Thanks for any help.

entry.title: Re: Multi-User Mode in mdk
entry.content: BrandedSettings.json is maintained at metadata folder level i.e. inside your .mdkproject. Refer this sample: https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-mdk-samples/tree/main/Showcase_Apps/PDFGenApp/pdfgenapp.mdkproject

Ctrl + Click on the localhost URL served will open the folder structure.
Then, Clicking on index.html will load the application.
